label: India / English
ladderNote: Urban Indian English from Indian Express, Times Now, and r/IndianTeenagers. Talking stage / situationship are common; arranged-marriage paths sit outside this ladder.

levels.meaning: Attraction or interest, nothing acted on yet.
Showing interest. "Propose" in Indian teen slang often means asking someone out, not marriage.
Regular texting / chatting with romantic intent; heavily used in Indian Gen Z dating talk.

levels.term: Hanging out / getting to know each other
levels.meaning: Spending time together without a defined label.
Casual sex, often one-off or low-commitment.
Friendship plus recurring sex; usually no romantic label.
Going on dates; may still see other people.
More than a hookup, less than a relationship. Covered extensively in Indian Express / Times Now for Indian readers.
Agreed not to see others; may still avoid boyfriend/girlfriend labels.
Defined romantic relationship with the label.
Serious couple language; often more adult than bf/gf.
Cohabiting as a couple (still socially uneven across India).
Formally planning marriage (love or arranged track).
